old_claim.note: Radio provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: GPS is a radio-navigation system whose satellites continuously transmit identification and navigation data on radio frequencies to user receivers.

source_shape.source_locator: Report pp. 1-3: calls GPS a space-based radio-navigation system and says its satellites continuously broadcast identification and navigation data on two radio frequencies.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The original NAVSTAR description places radio-frequency transmission between the satellite and receiver inside the system architecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Without the wireless signal link, the user segment cannot obtain the timing, identity, or navigation message used to compute range and position.

ontology_before: Radio was treated as a replaceable enabling option for GPS.
ontology_after: Radio transmission is a required direct prerequisite of the NAVSTAR satellite-to-user ranging link.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: radio remains required for gps.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to an offline data product that does not receive navigation signals from satellites.
short_reason: The contemporaneous GAO report defines NAVSTAR as a radio-navigation system with continuous satellite broadcasts.
